evidence_summary: Mission and patient-safety dependencies understood informally; not documented
No formal risk appetite or risk register; decisions ad hoc
HIPAA policies exist but last reviewed 3 years ago; no policy owner
Workstation inventory current; medical devices and cloud SaaS untracked
No documented HIPAA Security Risk Analysis on file - required by 164.308(a)(1)
AD/RBAC in place; deprovisioning manual; no periodic access review; MFA partial
Annual HIPAA training only; no phishing simulation for reset-request vishing
Laptops encrypted; server backups untested for restore; no DLP on email
Patching monthly for Windows; EHR vendor appliance unpatched 14 months
EHR audit logs retained but never reviewed; no alerting on PHI access
No defined thresholds for what constitutes a reportable anomaly
Downtime procedure exists for EHR outage; no security-specific IR plan
Breach notification duties unassigned; 60-day HIPAA clock risk
Backups run nightly; last restore test 18 months ago; RTO undefined
